verbatim_quote: Here, we report a detailed investigation of a P. aeruginosa H2-T6SS-dependent phospholipase effector, TplE, which acts as a trans-kingdom toxin. Delivery of TplE to the periplasmic space of rival bacteria leads to growth inhibition.
notes: Current DOI 10.1038/nature11600 is WRONG (Koga et al., protein-structure-design paper). PA1510 = Tle4 = TplE (Tli4 immunity = PA1509). Tle4 name from Russell 2013 (10.1038/nature12074) which lists PA1510 informatically but validated only Tle1/2/5. Primary experimental T6SS-secretion of PA1510 = Jiang et al. 2016 Cell Reports (PMID 27477276). DUPLICATE of idx 24 (same PA1510).

verbatim_quote: Consistent with this hypothesis, we identified a B. pseudomallei type III secreted protein, BopA, which plays a role in mediating bacterial evasion of autophagy.
notes: BopA is a Bsa TYPE III secretion effector, NOT T6SS. Misassigned. Current DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2024.04.012 (Szczesna 'Bacterial esterases reverse LPS ubiquitylation') is about TssM, only mentions BopA in a GFP panel. Primary: Cullinane et al. 2008 Autophagy (10.4161/auto.6246). Name-collision warning correct.

verbatim_quote: Here, we report a detailed investigation of a P. aeruginosa H2-T6SS-dependent phospholipase effector, TplE, which acts as a trans-kingdom toxin. Delivery of TplE to the periplasmic space of rival bacteria leads to growth inhibition.
notes: Current DOI 10.1016/j.celrep.2016.07.020 is WRONG (Liu 'MAPK ... Pulmonary Alveolar Regeneration'). Correct differs by last digits: 2016.07.012 (Jiang et al. 2016 Cell Reports, PMID 27477276), TplE=PA1510=Tle4 H2-T6SS effector. DUPLICATE of idx 15.

verbatim_quote: We report the characterization of BopE, a type III secreted protein that is encoded adjacent to the Burkholderia pseudomallei bsa locus and is homologous to Salmonella enterica SopE/SopE2.
notes: BopE is a Bsa TYPE III secretion effector (Cdc42/Rac1 GEF), NOT T6SS. Misassigned. Current DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2024.04.012 is about TssM, mentions BopE only in a GFP panel. Primary: Stevens et al. 2003 J Bacteriol (10.1128/JB.185.16.4992-4996.2003). Name-collision warning correct.

verbatim_quote: These data show that Tle1 is an antibacterial effector delivered between cells by T6S, and that I2699, henceforth referred to as Tli1 (type VI secretion lipase immunity 1), protects against Tle1.
notes: Tle1 = BTH_I2698 (B. thailandensis E264), T6SS-1 phospholipase effector. Current DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2012.04.007 (Russell 2012) is a real T6SS paper but does NOT name Tle1; the Tle nomenclature + experimental delivery come from Russell et al. 2013 Nature (10.1038/nature12074, PMID 23552891).
